title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
core_claim: MEME introduces a benchmark for multi-entity and evolving memory in LLM agents, and finds that existing memory systems fail badly on dependency-reasoning tasks like Cascade and Absence unless paired with impractical high-cost configurations.
triage_rationale: This looks like a solid benchmark paper with a clear gap analysis and concrete failure modes, which makes it useful for review. It is narrower than a breakthrough methods paper, but the controlled evaluation and practical cost tradeoff make it relevant and discussable.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark scope is limited to 100 controlled episodes
Claims about stronger models and prompt optimization depend on the specific tested configurations
